steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that Barrett and Rayfield suggested methodology significantly influences bite force estimates. To support this claim, the table data should demonstrate that different methodologies produce notably different results.

steps.title: Determine which choice demonstrates that methodology influences estimates
steps.content:
C describes how the two studies using muscular and skeletal modeling produced similar bite force ranges (Cost et al.: 35,000–63,000; Bates and Falkingham: 35,000–57,000), while studies using different methodologies produced substantially divergent results (Meers via body-mass scaling: 183,000–235,000; Gignac and Erickson via tooth-bone analysis: 8,000–34,000).
This clustering pattern directly demonstrates that methodology determines the estimates. A misidentifies the data points, B reports only one study's numbers without comparing methodologies, and D contradicts the suggestion by showing identical methodology producing different results.
This matches choice C — C is correct.
